name: Miranda Hernandez
bio: Miranda Hernandez, 34, is an attorney and daughter of state Representative Elizabeth 'Lisa' Hernandez (D-Cicero), deputy majority leader of the Illinois House and chair of the Illinois Democratic Party. Growing up, she is the child of Frank Aguilar's former political opponent — Aguilar defeated her mother in a 2002 race for an Illinois House seat. Miranda moved to Riverside, Illinois from Washington D.C. after Trump's inauguration in January 2025. In Washington she worked for a congresswoman and two senators, and served on the Senate Judiciary Committee staff and on the DOJ's Reproductive Rights Task Force under Biden. She holds joint J.D./MBA degrees from Northern Illinois University. She is currently a legislative deputy (and previously Deputy Director of Legislative Affairs) in the Cook County State's Attorney's Office. She has out-raised incumbent Aguilar and has the backing of powerful political figures and unions.

jacket.note: Raised more than $50,000 in 2025 per media reports, out-raising Aguilar. Elizabeth Hernandez's campaign committee transferred $20,000+ to Miranda's campaign fund. Illinois Sunshine committee URL not confirmed.

red_flags.label: Mother's Campaign Fund Paying Staffer Working Miranda's Campaign
red_flags.detail: Lisa Hernandez (Miranda's mother, Cicero Township Supervisor) has a campaign committee that is paying a Democratic Party of Illinois staffer who is also working on Miranda's campaign. Lisa Hernandez's committee also transferred $20K+ to Miranda's fund, raising pay-to-play and nepotism concerns.

policy_platform.position: Improve quality of life and maternal health outcomes
Fund law enforcement and public safety initiatives
Property tax assistance and appeals help for homeowners
Support small business access to grant funding
